picks.reason: Seattle has the better bullpen and Hancock has been effective, but Baltimore's starter is still TBD, which is a material uncertainty. My rough fair line is SEA -125, essentially matching the market. Total projects around 9.2 runs with warm weather, a favorable run environment, and Baltimore's weak season-long bullpen.

Boston owns a substantial platoon advantage versus left-handed pitching and Connelly Early's underlying metrics are stronger than his ERA estimators suggest. Ian Seymour has promising peripherals but remains a limited-sample arm with workload uncertainty. My fair line is closer to BOS -125.

The Yankees have the superior offense, far better run differential, and a starting pitching matchup that is close to even despite Gavin Williams' recent form. My fair line is NYY -115. Progressive Field suppresses offense and both bullpens remain strong enough to support a slight under lean.

Cristopher Sánchez has been one of the best starters on the slate and Philadelphia also holds the bullpen edge. My fair moneyline is around PHI -220 and the run line price is more attractive than laying heavy juice. The main uncertainty is unconfirmed lineups, but the edge is not driven solely by starting pitching.

Arrighetti's ERA is dramatically outperforming his underlying indicators while Grayson Rodriguez remains a small-sample, high-variance arm. Both starters carry significant uncertainty and the market appears close to fair. Offensive conditions point toward roughly 9.7 expected runs.

San Diego gets the better bullpen by a wide margin and Petco Park suppresses scoring. Walker Buehler's recent underlying form has improved, while Cincinnati enters on poor recent form with a vulnerable relief corps. My fair line is around SD -150.

Logan Webb's underlying metrics remain far stronger than his ERA and he draws an excellent matchup against Miles Mikolas. Oracle Park conditions include strong wind and a pitcher-friendly environment. My fair line is approximately SF -175.

Milwaukee owns major advantages in team quality, run differential, bullpen performance, and recent form. Kyle Harrison has been outstanding, but the Athletics profile well against lefties, preventing a larger stake. The hitter-friendly Sacramento environment supports an over lean near 11.7 projected runs.

parlay.reason: Both legs were independently identified as having value and rely on different matchup drivers. The games are not correlated and both favorites project several cents stronger than market pricing.

best_bet.why_this: Philadelphia has the clearest overall advantage on the slate, combining an elite starting pitcher, a meaningful talent gap, and a run-line price that is far more efficient than the heavy moneyline.
